page.home.title: shakti.ashram | Author-Led Retreats
page.home.hero.text: Author-led retreats and training programmes. A plunge into stillness, mindfulness practices and complete renewal.
page.home.directions.01.text: Pranayama, meditation, yoga — structured programmes for deepening your practice at your own pace.
page.home.directions.02.text: Individual session on energetics, Ayurveda and spiritual practices with a personal mentor.
page.home.directions.03.text: Immersive programmes in Montenegro, India and beyond. From 7 to 21 days of full immersion.
page.home.quote.line1: «Yoga is not what you do on the mat.
page.home.quote.line2: It is who you become — beyond it.»
page.home.formats.text1: Practice from anywhere in the world. Live sessions, recordings, personal feedback from your mentor at your own pace.
page.home.formats.text2: Real presence and deep connection. Retreats and intensives in sacred places of power.
page.home.reviews.1.quote: «After the Montenegro retreat I felt, for the first time in years, that my body and mind were in the same place. It was a true reset.»
page.home.reviews.1.name: Anna K.
page.home.reviews.2.quote: «The pranayama course changed my relationship with breathing — and with life itself. Simple techniques that work every single day.»
page.home.reviews.2.name: Mikhail R.
page.home.reviews.3.quote: «The Ayurveda consultation gave me a year-long plan — nutrition, daily routine, practices. Clear and without unnecessary mysticism.»
page.home.reviews.3.name: Svetlana V.
page.home.blog.1.h3: Pranayama for beginners: 5 techniques that will transform your sleep
page.home.blog.1.text: Simple breathing exercises you can do lying in bed — no mat or special equipment required.
page.home.blog.2.h3: Ahimsa in daily life: how the non-violence principle works off the mat
page.home.blog.2.text: How one of yoga’s foundational rules can be applied in conversations, diet and your relationship with yourself.
page.home.blog.3.h3: Why 7 days of silence changes more than a year of therapy
page.home.blog.3.text: Personal experience and analysis: what happens to the nervous system in conditions of deep silence and disconnection from everyday life.
page.home.cta.text: Leave a request — we’ll find the programme that’s right for you.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This document describes what data shakti.ashram collects, why it is needed for processing requests and client access, and who is responsible for data security after the project is handed over to the client.
privacy.contact: For data privacy questions: use the contact page on the website.
privacy.s1.title: 1. What data we collect
privacy.s1.p1: We collect only the data necessary for communication, manual lead qualification, and granting access to materials: name, phone, email, referral source, administrator notes, and access history.
privacy.s1.li1: The public request form saves contact details and the content of the request.
privacy.s1.li2: The administrator can add comments, status, and tags to the lead card.
privacy.s1.li3: After converting a lead to a client, the system stores the account, access history, and login records.
privacy.s2.title: 2. How the information is used
privacy.s2.p1: Data is used exclusively for project operations: contacting a potential client, making a decision on a request, granting access to premium materials or live sessions, and supporting the client within the scope of services provided.
privacy.s2.p2: The website does not use automated decision-making for leads. Access decisions are made by an administrator or manager authorized by the platform owner.
privacy.s3.title: 3. Where and how data is stored
privacy.s3.p1: Contact and operational data is stored in PostgreSQL. Media files may be stored locally or in S3-compatible object storage. Private materials use short-lived signed URLs.
privacy.s3.li1: The database contains lead, client, and access cards, as well as an audit log of administrative actions.
privacy.s3.li2: Object storage contains videos, documents, and other uploaded materials.
privacy.s3.li3: Database backups and recovery procedures must be maintained by the production infrastructure owner.
privacy.s4.title: 4. Who has access to the data
privacy.s4.p1: Only authorized project staff have access to data: CRM administrators, sales managers, and technical specialists maintaining the production environment. All critical admin actions must be recorded in the audit log.
privacy.s4.p2: Data transfer to third parties is permitted only for fulfilling client contracts, hosting, backup, or legal obligations.
privacy.s5.title: 5. Retention and deletion
privacy.s5.p1: Lead and client data is stored as long as needed for request processing, client support, financial reporting, and dispute resolution. Specific retention periods are set by the project owner in their operational policy.
privacy.s5.li1: Unclaimed leads should be regularly archived or deleted according to internal policy.
privacy.s5.li2: Terminated employees must have their CRM and infrastructure access revoked immediately.
privacy.s5.li3: Personal data deletion requests must be registered and fulfilled within the agreed timeframe.
privacy.s6.title: 6. Responsibility after project handover
privacy.s6.p1: After handing over the production environment to the client, responsibility for policy compliance, publishing current contacts, legal basis correctness, and operational security lies with the platform owner or their designated operator.
privacy.s6.p2: The contractor transfers code, deployment instructions, backup procedures, and support boundary, but does not remain a data controller automatically without a separate maintenance agreement.
privacy.s7.title: 7. Your rights as a data subject
privacy.s7.p1: Under GDPR and applicable legislation you have the right to:
privacy.s7.li1: Request access to your personal data and obtain a copy.
privacy.s7.li2: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data.
privacy.s7.li3: Request deletion of your data (right to be forgotten) if there is no lawful basis for retention.
privacy.s7.li4: Restrict or object to the processing of your data.
privacy.s7.li5: Receive your data in a structured format for transfer to another controller (data portability).
privacy.s7.p2: To exercise any of these rights, contact us via the contact page. We will process your request within 30 days.
privacy.s8.title: 8. Cookies
privacy.s8.p1: The website uses cookies to ensure proper operation and improve service quality.
privacy.s8.li1: Essential cookies: store language preference, cookie consent, and authorization token. Set without additional consent as they are required for the website to function.
privacy.s8.li2: Analytics cookies: may be used to collect anonymous visit statistics. Set only with your explicit consent.
privacy.s8.p2: You can change your choice at any time by clearing site data in your browser settings.
terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: This document describes the basic rules for using the public website, client dashboard, and protected materials of shakti.ashram. It defines access boundaries, user responsibilities, and the manual premium access model.
terms.contact: For organizational questions: use the contact page on the website.
terms.s1.title: 1. General provisions
terms.s1.p1: The public website provides information about practices, consultations, retreats, and other shakti.ashram products. Access to premium materials, private live sessions, and recordings is granted only after request processing and administrator approval.
terms.s1.p2: Submitting a request on the website does not constitute an automatic agreement and does not guarantee access without additional verification.
terms.s2.title: 2. Registration and client access
terms.s2.p1: A client account is created manually by an administrator based on a request or separate agreement with the user. The user must keep credentials secure and not share them with third parties.
terms.s2.li1: Access may be limited by duration, product type, or specific event.
terms.s2.li2: The user is responsible for actions performed from their account.
terms.s2.li3: If access compromise is suspected, the user must immediately notify the administrator.
terms.s3.title: 3. Content usage rules
terms.s3.p1: Platform materials are intended for personal use by the client within the granted access scope. Distribution of private recordings, documents, links to private files, and live sessions without the copyright holder's written consent is prohibited.
terms.s3.p2: Signed links to private media are time-limited. Attempts to bypass restrictions, share such links, or download content in violation of the rules constitute a breach of terms.
terms.s4.title: 4. Service limitation and access revocation
terms.s4.p1: The administrator may refuse, suspend, or revoke access if the user violates the terms of use, fails to fulfill payment agreements, or creates risks for other clients and platform infrastructure.
terms.s4.li1: Access revocation is recorded in the CRM with a clear reason.
terms.s4.li2: Critical changes to client access must be logged in the audit trail.
terms.s4.li3: Maintenance windows, outages, and scheduled work may temporarily restrict platform access.
terms.s5.title: 5. Limitation of liability
terms.s5.p1: The platform operates on a reasonable operational effort model. The team commits to maintaining service availability, backups, and production security, but is not liable for external service unavailability, network failures, infrastructure provider actions, or user violations.
terms.s5.p2: Platform practices and materials do not replace individual medical consultation unless explicitly stated in the agreement or service description.
terms.s6.title: 6. Changes to terms
terms.s6.p1: The platform owner may update the terms of use when the business model, service scope, infrastructure, or legal requirements change. The current version must be published on the website and used as the baseline reference version for client support.
